description: Frustration caused by long waits, rolling delays, missed connections, or extended time at the gate.
Problems with lost, delayed, misrouted, or poorly tracked baggage.
Helpful, empathetic, or professional staff behavior that shaped the customer experience.
Stress and disruption caused by cancellations, overnight stranding, and unclear next steps.
Lack of timely, accurate, or consistent updates during disruption and recovery.
Difficulty getting money back, understanding voucher policies, or securing reimbursement.
Friction in changing itineraries, standing by, or securing replacement seats after disruption.
Dissatisfaction with vouchers, reimbursement, extra spend, or perceived unfair compensation.
Responsiveness, delays, or friction in phone, chat, or email support channels.
Statements that the disruption damaged trust, repeat purchase intent, or brand loyalty.
Problems with self-service digital tools such as apps, websites, portals, and kiosks.
The airline ultimately resolved the disruption in a way the customer viewed positively.
